00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: Utility functions.
00029  * FILE:      util.c
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Utility routines needed by both the compiler and the interpreter.
00031  *
00032  *=======================================================================*/
00033 
00034 /*=========================================================================
00035  * Include files
00036  *=======================================================================*/
00037 
00038 #include "wrappers.h"
00039 
00040 #include "oobj.h"
00041 #include "utf.h"
00042 #include "sys_api.h"
00043 #include "path.h"       /* DIR_SEPARATOR */
00044 
00045 /*=========================================================================
00046  * Globals and extern declarations
00047  *=======================================================================*/
00048 
00049 extern struct StrIDhash *nameTypeHash;
00050 extern struct StrIDhash *stringHash;
00051 
00052 unicode *
00053 str2unicode(char *str, unicode *ustr, long len)
00054 {
00055     unicode *dst = ustr;
00056 
00057     memset((char *) dst, 0, len * sizeof(*dst));
00058     while (*str && --len >= 0)
00059     *ustr++ = 0xff & *str++;
00060     return dst;
00061 }
00062 
00063 void
00064 unicode2str(unicode *src, char *dst, long len)
00065 {
00066     while (--len >= 0)
00067     *dst++ = (char)(*src++);
00068     *dst = '\0';
00069 }
00070 
00071 int
00072 jio_printf (const char *format, ...)
00073 {
00074     int len;
00075 
00076     va_list args;
00077     va_start(args, format);
00078     len = jio_vfprintf(stdout, format, args);
00079     va_end(args);
00080 
00081     return len;
00082 }
00083 
00084 int
00085 jio_fprintf (FILE *handle, const char *format, ...)
00086 {
00087     int len;
00088 
00089     va_list args;
00090     va_start(args, format);
00091     len = jio_vfprintf(handle, format, args);
00092     va_end(args);
00093 
00094     return len;
00095 }
00096 
00097 int
00098 jio_vfprintf (FILE *handle, const char *format, va_list args)
00099 {
00100   return vfprintf(handle, format, args);
00101 }
00102 
00103 /*
00104  * Print s null terminated C-style character string.
00105  */
00106 void
00107 prints(char *s)
00108 {
00109     jio_fprintf(stdout, "%s", s);
00110 }
00111 
00112 #undef  HTSIZE            /* Avoid conflict on PC */
00113 #define HTSIZE 2003        /* Default size -- must be prime */
00114 #define HTOVERFLOWPOINT(h)    (h->size * 4 / 5)
00115 
00116 /*
00117  * We store most of the result of the hash in hash_bits to quickly
00118  * reject impossible matches because strcmp() is fairly expensive,
00119  * especially when many strings will have common prefixes.
00120  */
00121 typedef struct {
00122     char *hash;            /* the string for this entry */
00123     unsigned is_malloced :  1;    /* 1 if hash was malloced */
00124     unsigned hash_bits   : 31;    /* low 31 bits of the hash */
00125 } StrIDhashSlot;
00126 
00127 typedef void (*hash_fn)(const char *s, unsigned *h1, unsigned *h2);
00128 
00129 typedef struct StrIDhash {
00130     int size;            /* number of entries */
00131     hash_fn hash;        /* hash function for this table */
00132     struct StrIDhash *next;    /* next bucket */
00133     short used;            /* number of full entries */
00134     short baseid;        /* ID for item in slot[0] */
00135     void **params;        /* param table, if needed */
00136     StrIDhashSlot slot[1];    /* expanded to be number of slots */
00137 } StrIDhash;
00138 
00139 /* Hash a string into a primary and secondary hash value */
00140 static void
00141 default_hash(const char *s, unsigned *h1, unsigned *h2)
00142 {
00143     int i;
00144     unsigned raw_hash;
00145     for (raw_hash = 0; (i = *s) != '\0'; ++s)
00146 
00147     raw_hash = raw_hash * 37 + i;
00148     *h1 = raw_hash;
00149     *h2 = (raw_hash & 7) + 1; /* between 1 and 8 */
00150 }
00151 
00152 /* Create a hash table of the specified size */
00153 static StrIDhash *
00154 createHash(int entries)
00155 {
00156     StrIDhash *h;
00157     int size = offsetof(struct StrIDhash, slot) +  (entries * sizeof(h->slot));
00158     h = (StrIDhash *)sysCalloc(1, size);
00159     if (h != NULL) {
00160     h->size = entries;
00161     h->hash = default_hash;    /* only custom tables get something else */
00162     h->next = NULL;
00163     }
00164     return h;
00165 }
00166 
00167 /*
00168  * Given a string, return a unique 16 bit id number.
00169  * If param isn't null, we also set up an array of void * for holding info
00170  *     about each object.  The address of this void * is stored into param
00171  * If CopyNeeded is true, then the name argument "s" must be dup'ed, since
00172  * the current item is allocated on the stack.
00173  *
00174  * Note about returning 0 in the case of out of memory errors: 0 is *not*
00175  * a valid ID!  The out of memory error should be thrown by the calling code.
00176  */
00177 unsigned short
00178 Str2ID(StrIDhash **hash_ptr, register char *s, void ***param,
00179           int CopyNeeded)
00180 {
00181     /*
00182      * The database is a hash table.  When the hash table overflows, a new
00183      * hashtable is created chained onto the previous one.  This is done so
00184      * that we can use the hashtable slot index as the ID, without worrying
00185      * about having the IDs change when the hashtable grows.
00186      */
00187     StrIDhash *h = *hash_ptr;
00188     long i;
00189     unsigned primary_hash;
00190     unsigned secondary_hash;
00191     unsigned hash_bits = 0;
00192     hash_fn current_hash_func = NULL;
00193 
00194     if (h == NULL)
00195     goto not_found;
00196 
00197     /* Create the hash values */
00198     current_hash_func = h->hash;
00199     current_hash_func(s, &primary_hash, &secondary_hash);
00200     hash_bits = primary_hash & ((1u << 31) - 1);
00201 
00202     for (;;) {
00203     char *s2;
00204     int bucketSize = h->size;
00205 
00206     /* See if the new hash table has a different hash function */
00207     if (h->hash != current_hash_func) {
00208         current_hash_func = h->hash;
00209         current_hash_func(s, &primary_hash, &secondary_hash);
00210         hash_bits = primary_hash & ((1u << 31) - 1);
00211     }
00212     i = primary_hash % bucketSize;
00213 
00214     while ((s2 = h->slot[i].hash) != NULL) {
00215         if (h->slot[i].hash_bits == hash_bits && strcmp(s2, s) == 0)
00216         goto found_it;
00217         if ((i -= secondary_hash) < 0)
00218         i += bucketSize;
00219     }
00220     /* Not found in this table.  Try the next table. */
00221     if (h->next == NULL)
00222         break;
00223     h = h->next;
00224     }
00225 
00226 not_found:
00227     /* Either the hash table is empty, or the item isn't yet found. */
00228     if (h == NULL || (h->used >= HTOVERFLOWPOINT(h))) {
00229     /* Need to create a new bucket */
00230     StrIDhash *next;
00231         if (h && h->baseid > 30000 && *hash_ptr != stringHash) {
00232         panic("16-bit string hash table overflow");
00233     }
00234     next = createHash(HTSIZE);
00235     if (next == NULL) {
00236         /* Calling code should signal OutOfMemoryError */
00237         return 0;
00238     }
00239     if (h == NULL) {
00240         /* Create a new table */
00241         *hash_ptr = h = next;
00242         h->baseid = 1;  /* guarantee that no ID is 0 */
00243     } else {
00244         next->baseid = h->baseid + h->size;
00245         h->next = next;
00246         h = next;
00247     }
00248     if (h->hash != current_hash_func) {
00249         current_hash_func = h->hash;
00250         current_hash_func(s, &primary_hash, &secondary_hash);
00251         hash_bits = primary_hash & ((1u << 31) - 1);
00252     }
00253     i = primary_hash % h->size;
00254     }
00255     if (CopyNeeded) {
00256     char *d = strdup(s);
00257     if (d == NULL) {
00258         /* Calling code should signal OutOfMemoryError */
00259             return 0;
00260     }
00261     s = d;
00262     h->slot[i].is_malloced = 1;
00263     }
00264     h->slot[i].hash = s;
00265     h->slot[i].hash_bits = hash_bits;
00266     h->used++;
00267 
00268 found_it:
00269     /* We have found or created slot "i" in hash bucket "h" */
00270     if (param != NULL) {
00271     if (h->params == NULL) {
00272         h->params = sysCalloc(h->size, sizeof(void *));
00273         if (h->params == NULL) {
00274         /* Calling code should signal OutOfMemoryError */
00275         return 0;
00276         }
00277     }
00278     *param = &(h->params[i]);
00279     }
00280     return (unsigned short)(h->baseid + i);
00281 }
00282 
00283 /* Free an StrIDhash table and all the entries in it */
00284 void Str2IDFree(StrIDhash **hash_ptr)
00285 {
00286     StrIDhash *hash = *hash_ptr;
00287 
00288     while (hash != NULL) {
00289     StrIDhash *next = hash->next;
00290     StrIDhashSlot *ptr, *endPtr;
00291     for (ptr = &hash->slot[0], endPtr = ptr + hash->size;
00292               ptr < endPtr; ptr++) {
00293         if (ptr->is_malloced)
00294         sysFree(ptr->hash);
00295     }
00296     if (hash->params != NULL)
00297         sysFree(hash->params);
00298     sysFree(hash);
00299     hash = next;
00300     }
00301     *hash_ptr = 0;
00302 }
00303 
00304 /*
00305  * Call the callback function on every entry in the table.  This
00306  * should only be invoked holding the string hash table lock.
00307  */
00308 void Str2IDCallback(StrIDhash **hash_ptr, void (*callback)(char *, void *))
00309 {
00310     StrIDhash *hash, *next;
00311     int i;
00312 
00313     hash = *hash_ptr;
00314     while (hash) {
00315     void **params = hash->params;
00316     next = hash->next;
00317     for (i = 0; i < hash->size; i++) {
00318         if (hash->slot[i].hash != 0) {
00319         callback(hash->slot[i].hash, params ? params[i] : NULL);
00320         }
00321     }
00322     hash = next;
00323     }
00324 }
00325 
00326 /*
00327  * Returns NULL in the case of an error.
00328  */
00329 char *
00330 ID2Str(StrIDhash *h, unsigned short ID, void ***param)
00331 {
00332     int entry;
00333 
00334     while ((long)(ID - h->baseid) >= h->size) {
00335     h = h->next;
00336     }
00337     entry = ID - h->baseid;
00338     if (param != NULL) {
00339     if (h->params == NULL) {
00340         h->params = (void **)sysCalloc(h->size, sizeof(*param));
00341         if (h->params == NULL) {
00342         /* Calling code should signal OutOfMemoryError */
00343         return NULL;
00344         }
00345     }
00346     *param = &h->params[entry];
00347     }
00348     return h->slot[entry].hash;
00349 }
00350 
00351 char *
00352 addstr(char *s, char *buf, char *limit, char term)
00353 {
00354     char c;
00355     while ((c = *s) && c != term && buf < limit) {
00356     *buf++ = c;
00357     s++;
00358     }
00359     return buf;
00360 }
00361 
00362 char *
00363 unicode2rd(unicode *s, long len)
00364 {       /* unicode string to readable C string */
00365 #define CSTRLEN 40
00366     static char buf[CSTRLEN+1];
00367     char *dp = buf;
00368     int c = 0;
00369     if (s == 0)
00370     return "NULL";
00371     *dp++ = '"';
00372     while (--len>=0 && (c = *s++) != 0 && dp < buf + sizeof buf - 10)
00373     if (040 <= c && c < 0177)
00374         *dp++ = c;
00375     else
00376         switch (c) {
00377         case '\n':
00378         *dp++ = '\\';
00379         *dp++ = 'n';
00380         break;
00381         case '\t':
00382         *dp++ = '\\';
00383         *dp++ = 't';
00384         break;
00385         case '\r':
00386         *dp++ = '\\';
00387         *dp++ = 'r';
00388         break;
00389         case '\b':
00390         *dp++ = '\\';
00391         *dp++ = 'b';
00392         break;
00393         case '\f':
00394         *dp++ = '\\';
00395         *dp++ = 'f';
00396         break;
00397         default:
00398         /* Should not be possible to overflow, truncate if so */
00399         (void) jio_snprintf(dp, CSTRLEN+1 - (dp - buf), "\\%X", c);
00400         dp += strlen(dp);
00401         break;
00402         }
00403     *dp++ = '"';
00404     if (len >= 0 && c != 0)
00405     *dp++ = '.', *dp++ = '.', *dp++ = '.';
00406     *dp++ = 0;
00407     return buf;
00408 }
00409 
00410 /*
00411  * WARNING: out_of_memory() aborts the runtime!  It should not be used
00412  * except in the case of out of memory situations that are clearly not
00413  * survivable, like running out of memory before the runtime is initialized.
00414  * If the runtime has finished initializing and you run out of memory, you
00415  * should throw an OutOfMemoryError instead.
00416  */
00417 void
00418 out_of_memory()
00419 {
00420     jio_fprintf(stderr, "**Out of memory, exiting**\n");
00421         exit(1);
00422 }
00423 
00424 void
00425 panic(const char *format, ...)
00426 {
00427     va_list ap;
00428     char buf[256];
00429 
00430     printCurrentClassName();
00431     va_start(ap, format);
00432 
00433     /* If buffer overflow, quietly truncate */
00434     (void) jio_vsnprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), format, ap);
00435 
00436     jio_fprintf(stdout, "\nERROR: %s\n", buf);
00437 
00438     exit(1);
00439 }
00440 
00441 char *
00442 classname2string(char *src, char *dst, int size) {
00443     char *buf = dst;
00444     for (; (--size > 0) && (*src != '\0') ; src++, dst++) {
00445     if (*src == '/') {
00446         *dst = '.';
00447     }  else {
00448         *dst = *src;
00449     }
00450     }
00451     *dst = '\0';
00452     return buf;
00453 }
00454 
00455 typedef struct InstanceData {
00456     char *buffer;
00457     char *end;
00458 } InstanceData;
00459 
00460 #define ERROR_RETVAL -1
00461 #undef  SUCCESS
00462 #define SUCCESS 0
00463 #undef  CheckRet
00464 #define CheckRet(x) { if ((x) == ERROR_RETVAL) return ERROR_RETVAL; }
00465 
00466 static int
00467 put_char(InstanceData *this, int c)
00468 {
00469     if (iscntrl(0xff & c) && c != '\n' && c != '\t') {
00470         c = '@' + (c & 0x1F);
00471         if (this->buffer >= this->end) {
00472             return ERROR_RETVAL;
00473         }
00474         *this->buffer++ = '^';
00475     }
00476     if (this->buffer >= this->end) {
00477         return ERROR_RETVAL;
00478     }
00479     *this->buffer++ = c;
00480     return SUCCESS;
00481 }
00482 
00483 static int
00484 format_string(InstanceData *this, char *str, int left_justify, int min_width,
00485               int precision)
00486 {
00487     int pad_length;
00488     char *p;
00489 
00490     if (str == 0) {
00491     return ERROR_RETVAL;
00492     }
00493 
00494     if ((int)strlen(str) < precision) {
00495         pad_length = min_width - strlen(str);
00496     } else {
00497         pad_length = min_width - precision;
00498     }
00499     if (pad_length < 0)
00500         pad_length = 0;
00501     if (left_justify) {
00502         while (pad_length > 0) {
00503             CheckRet(put_char(this, ' '));
00504             --pad_length;
00505         }
00506     }
00507 
00508     for (p = str; *p != '\0' && --precision >= 0; p++) {
00509         CheckRet(put_char(this, *p));
00510     }
00511 
00512     if (!left_justify) {
00513         while (pad_length > 0) {
00514             CheckRet(put_char(this, ' '));
00515             --pad_length;
00516         }
00517     }
00518     return SUCCESS;
00519 }
00520 
00521 #define MAX_DIGITS 32
00522 
00523 static int
00524 format_number(InstanceData *this, long value, int format_type, int left_justify,
00525               int min_width, int precision, bool zero_pad)
00526 {
00527     int sign_value = 0;
00528     unsigned long uvalue;
00529     char convert[MAX_DIGITS+1];
00530     int place = 0;
00531     int pad_length = 0;
00532     static char digits[] = "0123456789abcdef";
00533     int base = 0;
00534     bool caps = false;
00535     bool add_sign = false;
00536 
00537     switch (format_type) {
00538       case 'o': case 'O':
00539           base = 8;
00540           break;
00541       case 'd': case 'D':
00542           add_sign = true; /* fall through */
00543       case 'u': case 'U':
00544           base = 10;
00545           break;
00546       case 'X':
00547           caps = true; /* fall through */
00548       case 'x':
00549           base = 16;
00550           break;
00551     }
00552     sysAssert(base > 0 && base <= 16);
00553 
00554     uvalue = value;
00555     if (add_sign) {
00556         if (value < 0) {
00557             sign_value = '-';
00558             uvalue = -value;
00559         }
00560     }
00561 
00562     do {
00563         convert[place] = digits[uvalue % (unsigned)base];
00564         if (caps) {
00565             convert[place] = toupper(convert[place]);
00566         }
00567         place++;
00568         uvalue = (uvalue / (unsigned)base);
00569         if (place > MAX_DIGITS) {
00570             return ERROR_RETVAL;
00571         }
00572     } while(uvalue);
00573     convert[place] = 0;
00574 
00575     pad_length = min_width - place;
00576     if (pad_length < 0) {
00577         pad_length = 0;
00578     }
00579     if (left_justify) {
00580         if (zero_pad && pad_length > 0) {
00581             if (sign_value) {
00582                 CheckRet(put_char(this, sign_value));
00583                 --pad_length;
00584                 sign_value = 0;
00585             }
00586             while (pad_length > 0) {
00587                 CheckRet(put_char(this, '0'));
00588                 --pad_length;
00589             }
00590         } else {
00591             while (pad_length > 0) {
00592                 CheckRet(put_char(this, ' '));
00593                 --pad_length;
00594             }
00595         }
00596     }
00597     if (sign_value) {
00598         CheckRet(put_char(this, sign_value));
00599     }
00600 
00601     while (place > 0 && --precision >= 0) {
00602         CheckRet(put_char(this, convert[--place]));
00603     }
00604 
00605     if (!left_justify) {
00606         while (pad_length > 0) {
00607             CheckRet(put_char(this, ' '));
00608             --pad_length;
00609         }
00610     }
00611     return SUCCESS;
00612 }
00613 
00614 int
00615 jio_vsnprintf(char *str, size_t count, const char *fmt, va_list args)
00616 {
00617     char *strvalue;
00618     long value;
00619     InstanceData this;
00620     bool left_justify, zero_pad, long_flag, add_sign, fPrecision;
00621     int min_width, precision, ch;
00622 
00623     if (str == NULL) {
00624     return ERROR_RETVAL;
00625     }
00626     str[0] = '\0';
00627 
00628     this.buffer = str;
00629     this.end = str + count - 1;
00630     *this.end = '\0';        /* ensure null-termination in case of failure */
00631 
00632     while ((ch = *fmt++) != 0) {
00633         if (ch == '%') {
00634             zero_pad = long_flag = add_sign = fPrecision = false;
00635             left_justify = true;
00636             min_width = 0;
00637             precision = this.end - this.buffer;
00638         next_char:
00639             ch = *fmt++;
00640             switch (ch) {
00641               case 0:
00642                   return ERROR_RETVAL;
00643               case '-':
00644                   left_justify = false;
00645                   goto next_char;
00646               case '0': /* set zero padding if min_width not set */
00647                   if (min_width == 0)
00648                       zero_pad = true;
00649               case '1': case '2': case '3':
00650               case '4': case '5': case '6':
00651               case '7': case '8': case '9':
00652                   if (fPrecision == true) {
00653                       precision = precision * 10 + (ch - '0');
00654                   } else {
00655                       min_width = min_width * 10 + (ch - '0');
00656                   }
00657                   goto next_char;
00658               case '.':
00659                   fPrecision = true;
00660                   precision = 0;
00661                   goto next_char;
00662               case 'l':
00663                   long_flag = true;
00664                   goto next_char;
00665               case 's':
00666                   strvalue = va_arg(args, char *);
00667                   CheckRet(format_string(&this, strvalue, left_justify,
00668                                          min_width, precision));
00669                   break;
00670               case 'c':
00671                   ch = va_arg(args, int);
00672                   CheckRet(put_char(&this, ch));
00673                   break;
00674               case '%':
00675                   CheckRet(put_char(&this, '%'));
00676                   break;
00677               case 'd': case 'D':
00678               case 'u': case 'U':
00679               case 'o': case 'O':
00680               case 'x': case 'X':
00681                   value = long_flag ? va_arg(args, long) : va_arg(args, int);
00682                   CheckRet(format_number(&this, value, ch, left_justify,
00683                                          min_width, precision, zero_pad));
00684                   break;
00685               default:
00686                   return ERROR_RETVAL;
00687             }
00688         } else {
00689             CheckRet(put_char(&this, ch));
00690         }
00691     }
00692     *this.buffer = '\0';
00693     return strlen(str);
00694 }
00695 
00696 int
00697 jio_snprintf(char *str, size_t count, const char *fmt, ...)
00698 {
00699     va_list args;
00700     int len;
00701 
00702     va_start(args, fmt);
00703     len = jio_vsnprintf(str, count, fmt, args);
00704     va_end(args);
00705     return len;
00706 }
00707 
00708 /* Return true if the two classes are in the same class package */
00709 
00710 bool
00711 IsSameClassPackage(ClassClass *class1, ClassClass *class2)
00712 {
00713     if (cbLoader(class1) != cbLoader(class2))
00714     return false;
00715     else {
00716     char *name1 = cbName(class1);
00717     char *name2 = cbName(class2);
00718     char *last_slash1 = strrchr(name1, DIR_SEPARATOR);
00719     char *last_slash2 = strrchr(name2, DIR_SEPARATOR);
00720     if ((last_slash1 == NULL) || (last_slash2 == NULL)) {
00721         /* One of the two doesn't have a package.  Only return true
00722          * if the other one also doesn't have a package. */
00723         return (last_slash1 == last_slash2);
00724     } else {
00725         int length1, length2;
00726         if (*name1 == SIGNATURE_ARRAY) {
00727         do name1++; while (*name1 == SIGNATURE_ARRAY);
00728         if (*name1 != SIGNATURE_CLASS) {
00729             /* Something is terribly wrong.  Shouldn't be here */
00730             return false;
00731         }
00732         name1++;
00733         }
00734         if (*name2 == SIGNATURE_ARRAY) {
00735         do name2++; while (*name2 == SIGNATURE_ARRAY);
00736         if (*name2 != SIGNATURE_CLASS) {
00737             /* Something is terribly wrong.  Shouldn't be here */
00738             return false;
00739         }
00740         name2++;
00741         }
00742         length1 = last_slash1 - name1;
00743         length2 = last_slash2 - name2;
00744         return ((length1 == length2)
00745             && (strncmp(name1, name2, length1) == 0));
00746     }
00747     }
00748 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:27 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
